---
title: "Gemini — Data Profile (consolidado)"
subtitle: "Visao agregada das 2 contas, com stacked bars por conta nos pontos-chave"
jupyter: python3
---

> **Documentos por conta** (mesmo template canonico das outras plataformas):
>
> - `gemini-acc-1.qmd` — `hello.marlonlemes@gmail.com` (47 convs)
> - `gemini-acc-2.qmd` — `marloonlemes@gmail.com` (33 convs)
>
> Este documento eh **consolidado** — agrega ambas e mostra distribuicoes
> empilhadas por conta nas secoes-chave (timeline, modelos, tamanho).

```{python}
#| label: setup
#| code-summary: "Setup — paths, conexao DuckDB, helpers"

import sys
from pathlib import Path

_this = Path.cwd()
_project_root = _this if (_this / "src").exists() else _this.parent
sys.path.insert(0, str(_project_root))

import json
import pandas as pd
import duckdb
import plotly.graph_objects as go
import plotly.express as px
from itables import init_notebook_mode, show

init_notebook_mode(all_interactive=False)

from src.parsers.quarto_helpers import setup_views_with_manual
PROCESSED = _project_root / "data" / "processed" / "Gemini"
con = duckdb.connect()
_views = setup_views_with_manual(
    con, "gemini", PROCESSED,
    ["conversations", "messages", "tool_events", "branches"],
)

# Google Gemini palette: azul + secundario
SOURCE_COLOR = "#4285F4"     # Google blue
SECONDARY = "#1A73E8"        # azul mais escuro
ACCENT = "#9AA0A6"           # cinza Google
MUTED = "#D1D5DB"


def fmt_pct(n, total):
    pct = n / total * 100 if total else 0
    return f"{n:,}/{total:,} ({pct:.0f}%)"


def show_df(df, **kwargs):
    from IPython.display import display
    display(df.style.hide(axis="index"))


def plotly_bar(x, y, title, color=SOURCE_COLOR, height=350, orientation="v"):
    if orientation == "h":
        fig = go.Figure(go.Bar(y=x, x=y, marker_color=color, orientation="h"))
    else:
        fig = go.Figure(go.Bar(x=x, y=y, marker_color=color))
    fig.update_layout(
        title=title, height=height, template="plotly_white",
        margin=dict(t=50, b=40, l=40, r=20),
    )
    return fig
```

# Visao geral

```{python}
#| label: overview

totals = con.execute("""
    SELECT
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM conversations) AS conversations,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM messages) AS messages,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tool_events) AS tool_events
""").df()

range_dt = con.execute("""
    SELECT
        MIN(created_at) AS oldest,
        MAX(created_at) AS newest_created,
        MAX(updated_at) AS newest_updated
    FROM conversations
""").df()

show_df(pd.DataFrame([
    {"Tabela": "conversations.parquet", "Rows": f"{totals['conversations'].iloc[0]:,}", "Periodo": f"{range_dt['oldest'].iloc[0]:%Y-%m-%d} → {range_dt['newest_created'].iloc[0]:%Y-%m-%d}"},
    {"Tabela": "messages.parquet", "Rows": f"{totals['messages'].iloc[0]:,}", "Periodo": "—"},
    {"Tabela": "tool_events.parquet", "Rows": f"{totals['tool_events'].iloc[0]:,}", "Periodo": "—"},
]))
```

> **Sobre este documento.** Catalogo descritivo dos parquets gerados pelo
> parser canonico do Gemini (`src/parsers/gemini.py`). Apresenta o que esta
> nos dados — schema, cobertura, distribuicoes, preservation. **Nao
> interpreta** conteudo (sem sentiment, clustering, topic detection). Pra
> analise qualitativa, ver projeto `~/Desktop/AI Interaction Analysis/`.
>
> Fonte: pasta unica cumulativa `data/merged/Gemini/account-{1,2}/` (output
> de `scripts/gemini-sync.py`). Schema raw eh **posicional** (Google
> batchexecute, sem keys) — caminhos descobertos empiricamente em probe
> 2026-05-02. Multi-conta: `conversation_id` namespace `account-{N}_{uuid}`
> pra evitar colisao entre contas.

# 1. Dados disponiveis

Cada tabela tem schema proprio. Aqui mostramos os campos, sua cobertura
nesta base e amostras diversificadas.

## 1.1 Conversations

Cada linha representa um chat do Gemini. `is_pinned` foi descoberto via
probe — vem do campo `c[2]` do listing MaZiqc (RPC do Google batchexecute).
Gemini **nao expoe `updated_at`** — usamos `max(turn timestamps)` como proxy.

```{python}
#| label: conv-schema

cov = con.execute("""
    SELECT
        COUNT(*) AS total,
        COUNT(title) AS title,
        COUNT(model) AS model,
        SUM(is_pinned::INT) AS pinned,
        SUM(is_preserved_missing::INT) AS preserved,
        COUNT(last_seen_in_server) AS last_seen,
        COUNT(settings_json) AS with_settings
    FROM conversations
""").df()
total = cov["total"].iloc[0]

def conv_cov(field):
    return fmt_pct(int(cov[field].iloc[0]), int(total))

schema_conv = pd.DataFrame([
    {"Campo": "conversation_id", "Tipo": "str", "Descricao": "Namespaced `account-{N}_<uuid>` pra evitar colisao multi-conta", "Cobertura": "100%"},
    {"Campo": "source", "Tipo": "str", "Descricao": "Sempre 'gemini'", "Cobertura": "100%"},
    {"Campo": "title", "Tipo": "str?", "Descricao": "Titulo do chat (vem do listing, nao do body)", "Cobertura": conv_cov("title")},
    {"Campo": "created_at", "Tipo": "timestamp", "Descricao": "`created_at_secs` do listing", "Cobertura": "100%"},
    {"Campo": "updated_at", "Tipo": "timestamp", "Descricao": "`max(turn[4][0])` — Gemini nao expoe updated_at, usamos max turn ts como proxy", "Cobertura": "100%"},
    {"Campo": "message_count", "Tipo": "int", "Descricao": "user + assistant msgs (cada turn = 2 msgs no Gemini)", "Cobertura": "100%"},
    {"Campo": "model", "Tipo": "str?", "Descricao": "Model display name do ultimo turn (e.g. '2.5 Flash', 'Nano Banana')", "Cobertura": conv_cov("model")},
    {"Campo": "account", "Tipo": "str", "Descricao": "'1' ou '2' (multi-conta Gemini)", "Cobertura": "100%"},
    {"Campo": "mode", "Tipo": "str", "Descricao": "Sempre 'chat' (Gemini nao distingue modes via API)", "Cobertura": "100%"},
    {"Campo": "url", "Tipo": "str", "Descricao": "Link `gemini.google.com/app/<short_id>`", "Cobertura": "100%"},
    {"Campo": "is_pinned", "Tipo": "bool", "Descricao": "Descoberto via probe — campo `c[2]` do listing MaZiqc", "Cobertura": f"{int(cov['pinned'].iloc[0])} flagged"},
    {"Campo": "is_preserved_missing", "Tipo": "bool", "Descricao": "True se conv sumiu do servidor (preservada local)", "Cobertura": f"{int(cov['preserved'].iloc[0])} flagged"},
    {"Campo": "last_seen_in_server", "Tipo": "timestamp", "Descricao": "Data da ultima run em que servidor retornou a conv", "Cobertura": conv_cov("last_seen")},
    {"Campo": "settings_json", "Tipo": "str?", "Descricao": "JSON com `locale` (e.g. 'BR') e `models_used` (lista distinct)", "Cobertura": conv_cov("with_settings")},
])
show_df(schema_conv)
```

```{python}
#| label: conv-sample
#| column: page

sample_conv = con.execute("""
    WITH
    pinned_one AS (
        SELECT * FROM conversations WHERE is_pinned LIMIT 1
    ),
    preserved AS (
        SELECT * FROM conversations WHERE is_preserved_missing LIMIT 1
    ),
    longest AS (
        SELECT * FROM conversations ORDER BY message_count DESC LIMIT 1
    ),
    nano_banana AS (
        SELECT * FROM conversations WHERE model LIKE 'Nano Banana%' LIMIT 1
    ),
    thinking_model AS (
        SELECT * FROM conversations WHERE model LIKE '%Thinking%' LIMIT 1
    ),
    acc1 AS (
        SELECT * FROM conversations WHERE account = '1' ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1
    ),
    acc2 AS (
        SELECT * FROM conversations WHERE account = '2' ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1
    )
    SELECT DISTINCT ON (conversation_id)
        substr(conversation_id, 1, 30) AS conv_id_preview,
        substr(title, 1, 40) AS title,
        account, message_count AS msgs, model,
        is_pinned, is_preserved_missing AS preserved,
        strftime(created_at, '%Y-%m-%d') AS created
    FROM (
        SELECT * FROM pinned_one
        UNION ALL SELECT * FROM preserved
        UNION ALL SELECT * FROM longest
        UNION ALL SELECT * FROM nano_banana
        UNION ALL SELECT * FROM thinking_model
        UNION ALL SELECT * FROM acc1
        UNION ALL SELECT * FROM acc2
    )
    ORDER BY conversation_id
""").df()
show_df(sample_conv)
```

## 1.2 Messages

Cada turn do Gemini gera 2 msgs (user + assistant). Schema raw eh
posicional — caminhos descobertos via probe:

| Campo | Path no raw |
|---|---|
| user text | `turn[2][0][0]` |
| assistant text | `turn[3][0][0][1]` (chunks juntados) |
| model | `turn[3][21]` |
| timestamp | `turn[4][0]` |
| thinking | `turn[3][0][0][37+]` (heuristica >=200 chars excl. main response) |
| image URLs | regex sobre JSON do turn (lh3.googleusercontent / gstatic) |

```{python}
#| label: msg-schema

mcov = con.execute("""
    SELECT
        COUNT(*) AS total,
        COUNT(model) AS model,
        COUNT(thinking) AS thinking,
        COUNT(asset_paths) AS asset_paths,
        COUNT(attachment_names) AS attachment_names,
        COUNT(content_types) AS content_types,
        SUM(CASE WHEN content_types = 'text+image' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS multi_modal
    FROM messages
""").df()
mtotal = mcov["total"].iloc[0]

def msg_cov(field):
    return fmt_pct(int(mcov[field].iloc[0]), int(mtotal))

schema_msg = pd.DataFrame([
    {"Campo": "message_id", "Tipo": "str", "Descricao": "`<conv_id>_t<idx>_<role>` (sequencial)", "Cobertura": "100%"},
    {"Campo": "conversation_id", "Tipo": "str", "Descricao": "FK pra conversations (com namespace de conta)", "Cobertura": "100%"},
    {"Campo": "sequence", "Tipo": "int", "Descricao": "Ordem cronologica dentro da conv", "Cobertura": "100%"},
    {"Campo": "role", "Tipo": "str", "Descricao": "'user' ou 'assistant' (Gemini nao tem tool/system msgs)", "Cobertura": "100%"},
    {"Campo": "content", "Tipo": "str", "Descricao": "Texto da msg (extracao posicional)", "Cobertura": "100%"},
    {"Campo": "model", "Tipo": "str?", "Descricao": "Model display name do turn (so em assistant msgs)", "Cobertura": msg_cov("model")},
    {"Campo": "thinking", "Tipo": "str?", "Descricao": "Thinking blocks juntados (heuristica de tamanho)", "Cobertura": msg_cov("thinking")},
    {"Campo": "created_at", "Tipo": "timestamp", "Descricao": "`turn[4][0]` (epoch secs)", "Cobertura": "100%"},
    {"Campo": "account", "Tipo": "str", "Descricao": "'1' ou '2' (herda da conv)", "Cobertura": "100%"},
    {"Campo": "content_types", "Tipo": "str", "Descricao": "'text' ou 'text+image' (assistant com image gen)", "Cobertura": msg_cov("content_types")},
    {"Campo": "asset_paths", "Tipo": "list[str]?", "Descricao": "Paths locais de imagens (resolvidos via assets_manifest.json)", "Cobertura": msg_cov("asset_paths")},
    {"Campo": "attachment_names", "Tipo": "str?", "Descricao": "JSON list de filenames (URL → filename)", "Cobertura": msg_cov("attachment_names")},
])
show_df(schema_msg)
```

```{python}
#| label: msg-sample
#| column: page

sample_msg = con.execute("""
    WITH
    user_msg AS (
        SELECT * FROM messages WHERE role='user' ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1
    ),
    asst_msg AS (
        SELECT * FROM messages WHERE role='assistant' AND thinking IS NULL ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1
    ),
    with_thinking AS (
        SELECT * FROM messages WHERE thinking IS NOT NULL ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1
    ),
    multi_modal AS (
        SELECT * FROM messages WHERE content_types = 'text+image' LIMIT 1
    ),
    nano_banana AS (
        SELECT * FROM messages WHERE model LIKE 'Nano Banana%' LIMIT 1
    )
    SELECT
        substr(message_id, 1, 40) AS msg_id,
        role,
        substr(content, 1, 50) AS content_preview,
        word_count AS wc,
        model,
        content_types,
        thinking IS NOT NULL AS has_thinking,
        asset_paths IS NOT NULL AS has_assets
    FROM (
        SELECT * FROM user_msg
        UNION ALL SELECT * FROM asst_msg
        UNION ALL SELECT * FROM with_thinking
        UNION ALL SELECT * FROM multi_modal
        UNION ALL SELECT * FROM nano_banana
    )
""").df()
show_df(sample_msg)
```

## 1.3 Tool Events

Operacoes nao-conversacionais. No Gemini, hoje cobrimos `image_generation`
— URLs `lh3.googleusercontent.com` extraidas via regex sobre o JSON do
turn, filtrando favicons/logos. Resolucao pra path local via
`assets_manifest.json` per account.

```{python}
#| label: evt-schema

ecov = con.execute("""
    SELECT
        COUNT(*) AS total,
        COUNT(tool_name) AS tool_name,
        COUNT(metadata_json) AS metadata_json
    FROM tool_events
""").df()
etotal = ecov["total"].iloc[0]

def evt_cov(field):
    return fmt_pct(int(ecov[field].iloc[0]), int(etotal))

schema_evt = pd.DataFrame([
    {"Campo": "event_id", "Tipo": "str", "Descricao": "`<msg_id>_img_<idx>` por imagem", "Cobertura": "100%"},
    {"Campo": "conversation_id", "Tipo": "str", "Descricao": "FK pra conversations", "Cobertura": "100%"},
    {"Campo": "message_id", "Tipo": "str", "Descricao": "ID da msg parent (assistant que gerou)", "Cobertura": "100%"},
    {"Campo": "event_type", "Tipo": "str", "Descricao": "'image_generation'", "Cobertura": "100%"},
    {"Campo": "tool_name", "Tipo": "str", "Descricao": "'gemini_image' (Nano Banana / Imagen)", "Cobertura": evt_cov("tool_name")},
    {"Campo": "metadata_json", "Tipo": "str", "Descricao": "JSON com `url`, `local_path`, `response_id`", "Cobertura": evt_cov("metadata_json")},
])
show_df(schema_evt)
```

```{python}
#| label: evt-sample

sample_evt = con.execute("""
    SELECT
        event_type,
        tool_name,
        substr(metadata_json, 1, 80) AS metadata_preview
    FROM tool_events
    LIMIT 5
""").df()
show_df(sample_evt)
```

# 2. Cobertura e gaps

Onde estao as lacunas — campos que nem sempre o servidor preenche, ou
artefatos que o parser nao consegue resolver completamente.

## 2.1 Convs sem `model`

`Conversation.model` sai do **ultimo model name visto** nos turns assistant.
Quando a conv eh somente user msg (em Gemini, raro porque assistant sempre
responde), o campo fica NULL.

```{python}
#| label: model-gap

no_model = con.execute("""
    SELECT
        strftime(created_at, '%Y-%m') AS mes,
        COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE model IS NULL) AS sem_model,
        COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE model IS NOT NULL) AS com_model
    FROM conversations
    GROUP BY mes ORDER BY mes
""").df()

fig = go.Figure()
fig.add_trace(go.Bar(x=no_model["mes"], y=no_model["com_model"],
                     name="Com model", marker_color=SOURCE_COLOR))
fig.add_trace(go.Bar(x=no_model["mes"], y=no_model["sem_model"],
                     name="Sem model", marker_color=MUTED))
fig.update_layout(
    barmode="stack", title="Convs por mes — com e sem model",
    height=350, template="plotly_white",
    xaxis=dict(type="category"),
    margin=dict(t=50, b=80, l=40, r=20),
    legend=dict(orientation="h", y=-0.2),
)
fig.show()
```

## 2.2 Cobertura de thinking (heuristica de extracao)

Thinking blocks vivem em `turn[3][0][0][37+]` — array nested. Heuristica
de extracao: blocos string `>=200` chars que NAO aparecem no main response
text (filtragem por exclusao). Conservadora — pode subestimar mas evita
falso positivo de duplicar response.

```{python}
#| label: thinking-coverage

think_cov = con.execute("""
    SELECT
        role,
        COUNT(*) AS total,
        COUNT(thinking) AS com_thinking,
        ROUND(COUNT(thinking) * 100.0 / COUNT(*), 1) AS pct
    FROM messages
    GROUP BY role ORDER BY role
""").df()
show_df(think_cov)
```

```{python}
#| label: thinking-by-model

think_by_model = con.execute("""
    SELECT
        model,
        COUNT(*) AS total_msgs,
        COUNT(thinking) AS com_thinking,
        ROUND(COUNT(thinking) * 100.0 / COUNT(*), 1) AS pct_thinking
    FROM messages
    WHERE role='assistant' AND model IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY model
    ORDER BY com_thinking DESC
""").df()

fig = px.bar(think_by_model, x="model", y="pct_thinking",
             title="% de assistant msgs com thinking — por modelo",
             color_discrete_sequence=[SOURCE_COLOR],
             height=350, text="pct_thinking")
fig.update_layout(template="plotly_white", margin=dict(t=50, b=80, l=40, r=20))
fig.update_xaxes(tickangle=-25)
fig.show()
show_df(think_by_model)
```

## 2.3 Asset paths resolvidos

Imagens geradas pelo Nano Banana (Gemini Image) tem URLs presigned em
`lh3.googleusercontent.com`. O `asset_downloader` baixa e mapeia em
`assets_manifest.json`. Quando o manifest nao tem o hash da URL,
`asset_paths` fica NULL.

```{python}
#| label: assets-coverage

asset_cov = con.execute("""
    SELECT
        content_types,
        COUNT(*) AS total,
        COUNT(asset_paths) AS com_paths,
        ROUND(COUNT(asset_paths) * 100.0 / COUNT(*), 1) AS pct
    FROM messages
    WHERE content_types = 'text+image'
    GROUP BY content_types
""").df()
show_df(asset_cov)
```

```{python}
#| label: asset-distrib

asset_count = con.execute("""
    SELECT len(asset_paths) AS n_assets, COUNT(*) AS n_msgs
    FROM messages WHERE asset_paths IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY n_assets ORDER BY n_assets
""").df()

if not asset_count.empty:
    fig = plotly_bar(
        x=asset_count["n_assets"].astype(str),
        y=asset_count["n_msgs"],
        title=f"Distribuicao de imagens por msg ({int(asset_count['n_msgs'].sum()):,} msgs com pelo menos 1 imagem)",
        height=300,
    )
    fig.update_xaxes(title="Imagens na msg")
    fig.update_yaxes(title="N msgs")
    fig.show()
```

## 2.4 ToolEvents — calls vs unique URLs

ToolEvents podem repetir mesma URL (uma msg referencia varias imagens, e
o mesmo asset pode aparecer em multiple msgs). Comparar count de events
vs URLs unicas mostra duplicidade.

```{python}
#| label: evt-vs-unique

evt_balance = con.execute("""
    SELECT
        COUNT(*) AS total_events,
        COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract_string(metadata_json, '$.url')) AS unique_urls
    FROM tool_events
""").df()
show_df(evt_balance)
```

# 3. Volumes e distribuicoes

Counts e timelines do que esta nos dados. **Sem interpretacao** — so o
"o que tem".

## 3.1 Conversas criadas por mes (stacked por conta)

```{python}
#| label: conv-timeline

timeline = con.execute("""
    SELECT strftime(created_at, '%Y-%m') AS mes, account, COUNT(*) AS n
    FROM conversations GROUP BY mes, account ORDER BY mes, account
""").df()
timeline["account"] = "account-" + timeline["account"].astype(str)

fig = px.bar(timeline, x="mes", y="n", color="account",
             title="Conversations criadas por mes (stacked por conta)",
             color_discrete_map={"account-1": SOURCE_COLOR, "account-2": SECONDARY},
             height=350)
fig.update_layout(barmode="stack", template="plotly_white",
                  xaxis=dict(type="category"),
                  margin=dict(t=50, b=80, l=40, r=20),
                  legend=dict(orientation="h", y=-0.2))
fig.show()
```

## 3.2 Mensagens por role

```{python}
#| label: roles

roles = con.execute("""
    SELECT role, COUNT(*) AS n FROM messages GROUP BY role ORDER BY n DESC
""").df()

fig = plotly_bar(x=roles["role"], y=roles["n"], title="Messages por role", height=300)
fig.show()
show_df(roles)
```

## 3.3 ToolEvents por event_type

```{python}
#| label: tool-events

evts = con.execute("""
    SELECT event_type, tool_name, COUNT(*) AS n
    FROM tool_events GROUP BY event_type, tool_name ORDER BY n DESC
""").df()

if not evts.empty:
    fig = plotly_bar(x=evts["event_type"], y=evts["n"],
                     title="ToolEvents por event_type", height=300)
    fig.show()
show_df(evts)
```

## 3.4 Modelos top (stacked por conta)

```{python}
#| label: top-models

top = con.execute("""
    SELECT CAST(model AS VARCHAR) AS model, account, COUNT(*) AS n
    FROM conversations
    WHERE model IS NOT NULL GROUP BY model, account
""").df()
order = (top.groupby("model")["n"].sum().sort_values(ascending=False)
         .head(10).index.tolist())
top_f = top[top["model"].isin(order)].copy()
top_f["account"] = "account-" + top_f["account"].astype(str)

fig = px.bar(top_f, x="model", y="n", color="account",
             title="Top modelos (last seen per conv) — stacked por conta",
             color_discrete_map={"account-1": SOURCE_COLOR, "account-2": SECONDARY},
             category_orders={"model": order},
             height=350)
fig.update_layout(barmode="stack", template="plotly_white",
                  margin=dict(t=50, b=80, l=40, r=20),
                  legend=dict(orientation="h", y=-0.2))
fig.update_xaxes(tickangle=-25)
fig.show()

pivot = (top.pivot_table(index="model", columns="account", values="n", fill_value=0)
         .assign(total=lambda d: d.sum(axis=1))
         .sort_values("total", ascending=False).reset_index())
pivot.columns = [f"acc-{c}" if c in ("1", "2") else c for c in pivot.columns]
show_df(pivot)
```

## 3.5 Modelos em assistant messages (stacked por conta)

```{python}
#| label: model-msgs

mod_msgs = con.execute("""
    SELECT CAST(model AS VARCHAR) AS model, account, COUNT(*) AS n
    FROM messages
    WHERE role = 'assistant' AND model IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY model, account
""").df()
order_m = (mod_msgs.groupby("model")["n"].sum().sort_values(ascending=False).index.tolist())
mod_msgs["account"] = "account-" + mod_msgs["account"].astype(str)

fig = px.bar(mod_msgs, x="model", y="n", color="account",
             title="Modelos por msg assistant — stacked por conta",
             color_discrete_map={"account-1": SOURCE_COLOR, "account-2": SECONDARY},
             category_orders={"model": order_m},
             height=350)
fig.update_layout(barmode="stack", template="plotly_white",
                  margin=dict(t=50, b=80, l=40, r=20),
                  legend=dict(orientation="h", y=-0.2))
fig.update_xaxes(tickangle=-25)
fig.show()
```

## 3.6 Distribuicao por conta

Multi-conta Gemini — convs ficam segregadas em `account-1` e `account-2`,
namespace `conversation_id` previne colisao.

```{python}
#| label: per-account

per_acc = con.execute("""
    SELECT account,
        COUNT(*) AS conversations,
        SUM(message_count) AS messages,
        SUM(is_pinned::INT) AS pinned,
        SUM(is_preserved_missing::INT) AS preserved
    FROM conversations GROUP BY account ORDER BY account
""").df()

fig = px.bar(per_acc, x="account", y="conversations",
             title="Convs por account",
             color_discrete_sequence=[SOURCE_COLOR],
             height=300, text="conversations")
fig.update_layout(template="plotly_white", margin=dict(t=50, b=40, l=40, r=20))
fig.show()
show_df(per_acc)
```

## 3.7 Distribuicao de tamanho (msgs por conv) — stacked por conta

```{python}
#| label: conv-size

sizes = con.execute("""
    SELECT account, message_count FROM conversations WHERE message_count > 0
""").df()

bins = [0, 1, 2, 5, 10, 20, 50, 100, 9999]
labels = ["1", "2", "3-5", "6-10", "11-20", "21-50", "51-100", "100+"]
sizes["bin"] = pd.cut(sizes["message_count"], bins=bins, labels=labels, include_lowest=True)
hist = sizes.groupby(["bin", "account"], observed=True).size().reset_index(name="n")
hist["bin"] = hist["bin"].astype(str)
hist["account"] = "account-" + hist["account"].astype(str)

fig = px.bar(hist, x="bin", y="n", color="account",
             title=f"Distribuicao de msgs por conv (n={len(sizes):,}) — stacked por conta",
             color_discrete_map={"account-1": SOURCE_COLOR, "account-2": SECONDARY},
             category_orders={"bin": labels},
             height=350)
fig.update_layout(barmode="stack", template="plotly_white",
                  margin=dict(t=50, b=80, l=40, r=20),
                  legend=dict(orientation="h", y=-0.2))
fig.update_xaxes(title="Msgs por conv")
fig.update_yaxes(title="N convs")
fig.show()
```

# 4. Preservation + features distintivas

## 4.1 Pinned (cross-platform)

`is_pinned` vem do listing MaZiqc — campo `c[2]`. Validado empirico em
2026-05-02 via probe pin-share.

```{python}
#| label: pinned-detail

pinned_table = con.execute("""
    SELECT
        substr(conversation_id, 1, 30) AS conv_id,
        title,
        account,
        message_count AS msgs,
        model,
        strftime(created_at, '%Y-%m-%d') AS created
    FROM conversations
    WHERE is_pinned = TRUE
    ORDER BY created_at DESC
""").df()

if not pinned_table.empty:
    show_df(pinned_table)
else:
    print("Nenhuma conv pinada nesta base.")
```

## 4.2 Preservation — convs sumidas do servidor

Quando uma conv eh deletada via UI, o reconciler marca
`is_preserved_missing=True` no merged. Body local + title sao mantidos
intactos; `last_seen_in_server` preserva data anterior ao delete.

```{python}
#| label: preserved-detail

preserved_table = con.execute("""
    SELECT
        substr(conversation_id, 1, 30) AS conv_id,
        title,
        account,
        message_count AS msgs,
        strftime(last_seen_in_server, '%Y-%m-%d') AS last_seen,
        strftime(created_at, '%Y-%m-%d') AS created
    FROM conversations
    WHERE is_preserved_missing = TRUE
    ORDER BY last_seen_in_server DESC
""").df()

if not preserved_table.empty:
    show_df(preserved_table)
else:
    print("Nenhuma conv preservada (sem deletes detectados).")
```

## 4.3 Multi-modal coverage (image generation)

```{python}
#| label: multi-modal

mm = con.execute("""
    SELECT
        CASE WHEN content_types = 'text+image' THEN 'multi-modal'
             ELSE 'text-only' END AS tipo,
        COUNT(*) AS msgs
    FROM messages WHERE role = 'assistant'
    GROUP BY tipo ORDER BY msgs DESC
""").df()

fig = px.pie(mm, values="msgs", names="tipo",
             title="Assistant messages — text vs multi-modal",
             color_discrete_sequence=[SOURCE_COLOR, MUTED],
             height=350)
fig.update_layout(template="plotly_white", margin=dict(t=50, b=40, l=40, r=20))
fig.show()
```

# 5. Limitacoes conhecidas

| Item | Status |
|---|---|
| Branches/drafts (turn[1] tem 2 response_ids alternados) | nao implementado v3 — poucos casos detectados |
| Search/grounding citations | estrutura nao mapeada (1/80 convs com 'grounding' substring — TODO probe) |
| Deep Research markdown reports | extraidos offline pelo `asset_downloader`, surfaced em `assets/` (~18 reports nesta base) |
| `updated_at` real | Gemini nao expoe — usa max(turn timestamps) como proxy |
| Voice / TTS audio | nao identificado em probe (provavel `resp[12]`?) |
| Share URLs (`gemini.google.com/share/<id>`) | upstream-only — nao modifica state do chat, nao gap do extractor |
| Add to notebook (integracao NotebookLM) | nao testado |
